idea.hook: How long does it take to *really* leave our Solar System?
facts: The Oort Cloud, our Solar System's icy outer shell, begins ~300 billion km away.
Our fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, travels at ~17 km/s.
At this speed, reaching the *inner* Oort Cloud would take over 550 years.
Crossing its full 15-trillion-km expanse (up to 100,000 AU) would take over 28,000 years!
Even at the speed of light, traversing the *entire* Oort Cloud takes over 1.5 years!
idea.payoff: Our cosmic neighborhood is vastly larger than we imagine, even for light!
idea.title: Leaving Our Solar System's TRUE Edge! 🚀⏳

scenes.text: How long to REALLY leave our Solar System?
scenes.visual: A stylized sun and planets shrink, replaced by a vast, starry expanse with a large, animated '?' appearing.

scenes.text: Oort Cloud: Starts ~300 BILLION km out.
scenes.visual: Animation zooms out from a familiar solar system, revealing a faint, vast, spherical cloud of icy bodies surrounding it. The distance '300 BILLION km' is highlighted.

scenes.text: Voyager 1 (17 km/s) to INNER Oort: 550+ YEARS!
scenes.visual: A small, stylized spacecraft (like Voyager) slowly moves across the screen from left to right against a dark, star-speckled background. A digital counter rapidly increments, settling on '550+ YEARS'.

scenes.text: Crossing the FULL Oort Cloud: 28,000+ YEARS!
scenes.visual: The Voyager icon continues its journey, now appearing even smaller against an infinitely vast, darker expanse. The counter accelerates dramatically and displays '28,000+ YEARS'.

scenes.text: Even LIGHT takes 1.5 YEARS to cross! Our neighborhood is VAST.
scenes.visual: A bright, fast beam of light streaks across the dark, icy Oort Cloud, quickly traversing the immense distance. The scene then transitions to a mesmerizing view of a distant, beautiful galaxy, emphasizing cosmic scale.

script.thumbnail_text: Leave Our Solar System? Think Again!

youtube.title: Leaving Our Solar System's TRUE Edge! 🚀⏳
